hero.subtitleBody: lets you lend and borrow tokens, rent NFTs, and set your own terms. Every position is tracked by a unique NFT — transparent, traceable and trustless from offer to settlement.

features.title: Everything you need for trustless lending
features.subtitle: A complete DeFi lending protocol built on the Diamond Standard, supporting ERC-20 tokens and rentable NFTs across Ethereum, Polygon, and Arbitrum.
features.p2pDesc: Create or accept offers for ERC-20 tokens with custom terms — interest rate, collateral type, and loan duration. No intermediary pools.
features.nftDesc: Rent out ERC-721 and ERC-1155 NFTs with daily pricing. Borrowers get user rights while the NFT stays safely in the vault.
features.vaultDesc: Every user gets their own UUPS upgradeable vault proxy. No fund commingling — assets are always isolated.
features.nftPositionDesc: Every offer and loan mints a unique Vaipakam NFT with on-chain metadata. Prove ownership, claim funds, and track status on any marketplace.
features.liquidityDesc: Chainlink oracles + on-chain DEX liquidity checks determine asset status. Liquid assets get LTV monitoring; illiquid assets use full collateral transfer.
features.liquidationDesc: Health Factor drops below threshold? Permissionless DEX liquidation kicks in. Grace period expired? Time-based default with collateral transfer.
features.precloseDesc: Borrowers can early-repay, transfer debt to another borrower, or offset with a new lender position — all while protecting the original lender.
features.alertsDesc: Platform-funded SMS and email alerts for LTV warnings, repayment reminders, liquidation events, and claimable funds.

howItWorksSection.title: From offer to settlement in four steps
howItWorksSection.subtitle: Every action is recorded on-chain. Position NFTs provide proof of ownership and are required to claim funds.
howItWorksSection.step1Desc: As a lender, specify the asset, interest rate, collateral requirements, and duration. As a borrower, state what is needed and the collateral that can be provided. Assets are locked in the Vaipakam Vault.
howItWorksSection.step1Detail3: Vaipakam NFT minted for the position
howItWorksSection.step2Desc: Browse the offer book, filter by asset type or rate, and accept a compatible offer. The accepting party pays gas. Collateral is locked, funds transfer, and both parties receive position NFTs.
howItWorksSection.step3Desc: Monitor the position via the dashboard. Add collateral if LTV rises. Withdraw excess if Health Factor is strong. Get notified before critical thresholds are breached.
howItWorksSection.step4Desc: Repay on time and reclaim the collateral. Lenders claim principal + interest using the NFT. If default occurs, liquidation or full collateral transfer follows the protocol rules.
howItWorksSection.step4Detail3: 0.1% Loan Initiation Fee + 1% Yield Fee on interest
howItWorksSection.step4Detail4: Tiered VPFI discount: 10% / 15% / 20% / 24% off both fees by vault balance

security.title: Built for safety at every layer
security.subtitle: From smart contract architecture to liquidation mechanics, every design decision prioritizes the safety of assets. Each card below links to the on-chain artifact backing the claim — verify the standard yourself.
security.diamondDesc: Modular proxy architecture. Upgrade individual facets without disrupting the protocol. All calls route through a single diamond entry point.
security.vaultDesc: Each user gets a dedicated UUPS proxy vault. No commingling of funds. Mandatory vault upgrades block interactions until the user upgrades.
security.transparencyDesc: Every offer, loan, and status change is recorded on-chain. Vaipakam NFTs serve as verifiable proof of position ownership.
security.slippageDesc: DEX liquidation aborts if slippage exceeds 6%. Falls back to full collateral transfer via NFT claim model. No forced bad swaps.
security.auditDesc: Built on OpenZeppelin contracts. ReentrancyGuard and Pausable on all facets. Mandatory third-party audits before mainnet deployment.
security.kycDesc: Vaipakam is non-custodial and does not require KYC. Users are responsible for their own regulatory compliance. Tiered-KYC and country-pair logic remains in the codebase for future governance activation if a separate industrial deployment ever needs it.

cta.title: Ready to start lending?
cta.subtitle: Connect a wallet, create an offer, and start earning. Set custom terms — no pool, no middleman.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about lending, borrowing, and building on Vaipakam.
faq.empty: No questions match "{{query}}". Try a different term.

basics-what-is-vaipakam.q: What is Vaipakam, in plain English?
basics-what-is-vaipakam.a1: Vaipakam is a vault-to-vault marketplace for lending crypto and renting digital items (NFTs). One person posts an offer with the terms they're comfortable with — amount, interest, duration — and another person accepts it. No bank, no fund manager, no intermediary sitting between the two counterparties.
basics-what-is-vaipakam.a2: What keeps everyone honest is a set of smart contracts on a blockchain. They hold the assets in individual vault during the loan and release them automatically when the terms are met. Each user remains in control of their own funds; the protocol simply enforces the deal that was agreed.

basics-wallet.q: Do I need a crypto wallet, and what is one?
basics-wallet.a1: A wallet (MetaMask, Rabby, Coinbase Wallet, and similar) is the app that holds the private key controlling on-chain assets. Think of it as a login and a signing pen rolled into one — it's how Vaipakam knows that a transaction is genuinely coming from the account holder.
basics-wallet.a2: A wallet is only required to act on a position (create an offer, accept a loan, repay, claim). Browsing the analytics dashboard and reading loan details works with no wallet at all. Vaipakam never sees or stores private keys — the wallet lives entirely in the browser or extension.

basics-collateral.a1: Collateral is an asset locked up for the duration of the loan to guarantee repayment. Because the lender does not know the borrower personally, they need a credible commitment that the borrower will not simply walk away with the borrowed funds.
basics-collateral.a2: Every loan on Vaipakam is <s>over-collateralized</s>, meaning the collateral posted is worth more than the amount borrowed. If the loan is repaid as agreed, the borrower gets it all back, no strings attached. If not, the lender has a protocol-enforced path to claim it.

basics-chains.q: Why are there so many different blockchains (Base, Polygon, Arbitrum…)?
basics-chains.a1: A blockchain is the shared ledger that records every transaction. Different blockchains — Ethereum, Base, Polygon, Arbitrum, Optimism — are independent ledgers, each with its own fees, speed, and set of available tokens. They do not automatically share state.
basics-chains.a2: Vaipakam runs on several of these, and a single loan lives entirely on one chain from start to finish. The chain selector lets the user choose which one to read or transact on. Picking a cheaper L2 like Base or Polygon usually means paying much lower gas fees than Ethereum mainnet.

basics-apr.q: What does "APR" mean on a loan offer?
basics-apr.a1: APR stands for <s>Annual Percentage Rate</s> — the interest rate stated on a yearly basis. A loan with a 10% APR over a full year would cost the borrower 10% of the principal in interest; the same 10% APR over six months costs roughly 5%, and over a month roughly 0.83%.
basics-apr.a2: On the offer screen, the APR and the duration are always shown side by side so the user can sanity-check the total cost before accepting. The exact accrual formula is fixed at the moment the loan starts and cannot be changed afterwards.

basics-position-nft.q: Why was I given an NFT when my loan started?
basics-position-nft.a1: The role in a loan — as lender or borrower — is represented as an NFT instead of an entry in a database. It's essentially a permanent, transferable receipt: it proves which side of the loan the holder is on and is what they present to claim funds later.
basics-position-nft.a2: Because this receipt is an NFT, it is also transferable. Lenders who want to exit early can sell the position to another lender without disturbing the borrower; the loan keeps running unchanged, and the new holder simply takes over the right to be repaid.

basics-gas.q: What are "gas fees", and why does every action cost a little?
basics-gas.a1: Gas is the fee the blockchain itself charges to process a transaction. It pays the network of computers that validate the transaction and add it to the ledger. It is paid in the chain's native token — ETH on Ethereum or Base, MATIC on Polygon, and so on.
basics-gas.a2: Vaipakam never takes a cut of gas — it goes straight to the network. Using an L2 (Base, Arbitrum, Optimism, Polygon) usually keeps gas at a few cents per action, which is why most users prefer them over Ethereum mainnet for day-to-day activity.

basics-custody.q: Who actually holds my money while a loan is active?
basics-custody.a1: Nobody holds it in the traditional sense. Assets live in a smart-contract vault that only the user and the protocol's own rules can move. Each user has their own isolated vault — funds are never pooled with strangers — and every release of those funds follows a rule that was published on-chain before the user ever clicked "accept".
basics-custody.a2: The contracts are open source and have been audited. Vaipakam cannot freeze, redirect, or claw back any balance; the most the team can do is pause new actions in an emergency. Repayment, claim, or exit always remains available.

basics-vpfi-intro.q: What is VPFI, and do I need any to use the app?
basics-vpfi-intro.a1: VPFI is Vaipakam's native platform token. It is <s>not</s> required to lend or borrow — the app works entirely without it, and every core feature is open to anyone who shows up.
basics-vpfi-intro.a2: Where VPFI becomes useful is for regular users. Holding it in the Vaipakam Vaults grants a discount on platform fees and lets the holder earn passive rewards on top. Think of it as an optional loyalty layer, not a gate.

assets.q: What assets can I lend or borrow?
assets.a1: Vaipakam supports two loan shapes: ERC-20 loans (lend a fungible token) and NFT rentals (rent out an ERC-721 or ERC-1155).
assets.li1: <s>ERC-20 loans:</s> principal is any ERC-20 (USDC, ETH, WBTC, etc.); collateral can be any ERC-20 or any NFT.
assets.li2: <s>NFT rentals:</s> the rented NFT is the principal; collateral is an ERC-20. Native ERC-4907 on the NFT is optional — the Vaipakam Vault wrapper exposes a uniform <c>userOf</c> / <c>userExpires</c> / <c>userQuantity</c> surface regardless.

vault.q: How does the Vaipakam Vault work?
vault.a1: Every user gets their own UUPS upgradeable proxy vault, deployed on first use by the <c>VaultFactoryFacet</c> (clone-factory pattern). Assets are never commingled with anyone else's.
vault.a2: The Diamond orchestrates moves across vaults, but claim rights are tied to the Vaipakam position NFT — so vault custody is strictly constrained by on-chain position state.

default.q: What happens if a borrower defaults?
default.a1: There are two distinct liquidation paths:
default.li1: <s>Health Factor trigger:</s> if HF drops below 1.0 (while the loan is still live), anyone can call the permissionless DEX liquidation. The router swaps collateral via 0x with a 6% max slippage, and the liquidator receives a bonus.
default.li2: <s>Time-based trigger:</s> once the grace period expires, liquid collateral is swapped to repay the lender; illiquid collateral (NFTs, or tokens without a qualifying oracle + v3-style concentrated-liquidity AMM pool) is transferred directly to the lender via the NFT-claim model.
default.a2: If a DEX swap cannot meet the 6% slippage bound, the fallback route delivers collateral-equivalent value to the lender and splits a premium (3% to the lender, 2% to treasury) as a distress bonus.

early-exit.q: Can I exit my position early?
early-exit.a1: Yes — both sides have exits that protect the counterparty's economics:
early-exit.li1: <s>Borrower early repayment:</s> pay principal plus the full term's accrued interest at any time; the lender still receives the yield they were promised.
early-exit.li2: <s>Debt transfer / offset:</s> hand the debt to another borrower, or create an offsetting new position that nets down exposure without touching the lender.
early-exit.li3: <s>Lender early withdrawal / sale:</s> sell the position NFT to another lender, or post an offsetting offer. The underlying loan keeps running unchanged for the borrower.

durations.q: How are loan durations and grace periods determined?
durations.a1: Loan durations are configurable between 1 day and 1 year. Grace periods are derived from the term:

chains.a1: Phase 1 targets <s>Base</s>, <s>Polygon</s>, <s>Arbitrum</s>, <s>Optimism</s>, and <s>Ethereum mainnet</s>. Base is the canonical chain for VPFI (where the token is minted and the fixed-price buy lives), but the in-app Buy VPFI page works from every supported chain — it auto-selects a direct buy on Base, or a CCIP-bridged buy from any mirror chain, so the user never has to switch networks just to top up.
chains.a2: The live deployment today is <s>Sepolia</s> while additional chains are being rolled out. All aspects of a single loan must occur on the same network.

sequencer.q: What if the L2 sequencer goes down?
sequencer.a1: On supported L2s, the protocol reads the Chainlink sequencer uptime feed. If the sequencer is reported down, or has been back up for less than a 1-hour grace window, oracle-sensitive actions — new loans, HF-based liquidations, and rate-dependent quotes — fail-closed. Repayments and user-initiated exits keep working so a user can never be trapped in a position.

liquidity-status.q: How does Vaipakam decide if an asset is liquid or illiquid?
liquidity-status.a1: Liquidity is judged <s>only on the network being transacted on</s>. The protocol checks two things on that chain: a reliable Chainlink price path for the asset, and a sufficiently deep v3-style concentrated-liquidity AMM <c>asset/WETH</c> 0.3% pool. If either fails, the asset is marked <s2>illiquid on that network</s2>.
liquidity-status.a2: There is no Ethereum-mainnet fallback. An asset that's liquid on mainnet but not on, say, Base is simply illiquid on Base — the protocol will not reach back to mainnet to justify liquid treatment. If the asset is illiquid, LTV / Health Factor checks are skipped (the collateral is valued at $0), and both parties must accept the combined abnormal-market + illiquid-assets risk acknowledgement before the offer can proceed.

preclose-refinance.q: What are preclose, refinance, and lender early-withdrawal?
preclose-refinance.a1: Beyond regular repayment, there are three strategic-exit flows:
preclose-refinance.li1: <s>Borrower preclose:</s> the borrower settles the loan ahead of schedule. While the preclose flow is in progress, the borrower-side position NFT is <s2>locked for transfer</s2> until the flow completes, is cancelled, or is unwound — a notice about this appears in the transaction review before confirmation.
preclose-refinance.li2: <s>Lender early-withdrawal:</s> the lender exits the loan before its natural end. Same transfer-lock rules apply to the lender-side NFT for the duration of the flow.
preclose-refinance.li3: <s>Refinance:</s> the borrower swaps one active loan for a fresh loan with a new lender. By the time the borrower signs the refinance transaction, the replacement lender has already accepted the new offer and the replacement loan already exists — the on-chain refinance call is a single atomic settlement, so there is no refinance-specific NFT lock to worry about.

keepers.q: What are keepers, and can they take my assets?
keepers.a1: Keepers are optional <s>delegated role-managers</s> — they can execute the on-chain role assigned to them (for example, calling <c>repayLoan</c> on the user's behalf or triggering a default settlement), but they <s2>cannot claim assets</s2>. Claims are always locked to whoever currently holds the relevant Vaipakam position NFT, which is the user unless they have transferred it.
keepers.a2: Enabling a keeper takes <s>two opt-ins</s>, by design:
keepers.li1: <s>User-level:</s> in advanced settings, the user must turn on keeper access for their side (borrower or lender) and add the keeper to a whitelist.
keepers.li2: <s>Position-level:</s> the offer (or accept) flow must have keeper execution enabled for that specific position.
keepers.a3: If either layer is off, the keeper cannot act — flipping the position-level flag alone is not enough. Keeper access for an existing loan can also be enabled or disabled later, from advanced settings, for the user's own side. Keeper controls only appear in <s>Advanced mode</s>; they are intentionally hidden from the default everyday workflow.

fees.q: What fees does the platform charge?
fees.a1: Two headline fees, plus a late-payment schedule:
fees.li1: <s>Loan Initiation Fee — 0.1%</s> on ERC-20 loans, deducted from the lender's principal at loan start. The borrower receives 99.9% of the offered amount but still owes the full principal.
fees.li2: <s>Yield Fee — 1%</s> on interest earned by lenders and on rental income earned by NFT owners.
fees.li3: <s>Late fees:</s> 1% flat on the day of miss, plus 0.5% per additional day, capped at 5% of principal. Late fees route to treasury.
fees.a2: VPFI holders can reduce both headline fees via a single platform-level consent — see the VPFI discount entry below.

vpfi-discount.q: How does the VPFI discount work?
vpfi-discount.a1: VPFI held in the Vaipakam Vaults automatically places the holder in a discount tier. One platform-level consent covers both the borrower (initiation) and lender (yield) legs, and the discounted fee is auto-deducted in VPFI from the vault — the borrower then receives the full principal and the lender keeps the full interest on the discount path.

nft-positions.a1: Every offer and every loan mints a unique ERC-721 with on-chain metadata (asset types, amounts, rates, status). The NFT is the position — it proves ownership and is required to claim funds after repayment, default, or liquidation. Because metadata is on-chain, any standard NFT marketplace can render the current state.

integrator-rental.q: How do I read rental state for a Vaipakam-rented NFT?
integrator-rental.a1: Query the lender's Vaipakam Vaults wrapper — not the underlying NFT — through the Diamond. For ERC-1155 rentals with multiple concurrent renters on the same (nftContract, tokenId), vaultGetNFTQuantity returns the active aggregate quantity and vaultGetNFTUserExpires returns the minimum active expiry.
integrator-rental.a2: The wrapper maintains authoritative rental state even when the underlying NFT does <s>not</s> implement ERC-4907, so integrators get a uniform surface across every rented asset.

integrator-subgraph.q: What loan-lifecycle events can I index?
integrator-subgraph.a1: The Diamond emits a stable set of lifecycle events that the reference subgraph indexes. Subscribe to these directly, or query the subgraph:
integrator-subgraph.li3: <c>LoanDefaulted</c> — grace period elapsed, claim path unlocked
integrator-subgraph.li5: <c>LiquidationFallback</c> — DEX path blocked; collateral-equivalent + premium paid out

vaultAssets.pageSubtitle: Tokens held by the protocol on your behalf. Each user has their own isolated vault proxy — deposits and withdrawals always flow through the Vaipakam Diamond.
vaultAssets.addressCaption: Internal protocol storage — funds enter and exit via the protocol only.
vaultAssets.noVaultYet: You don't have a vault yet. One will be created automatically the first time you create or accept an offer.
vaultAssets.lowShowAll: Show all balances including zero / untracked / dust
vaultAssets.lowHide: Hide zero / untracked / dust balances (anything below 1×10⁻¹¹ in display units)
vaultAssets.noProtocolTokensOnChain: No protocol-managed tokens are configured on this chain yet.
vaultAssets.fullyLocked: Fully locked — backing open loans / offers
vaultAssets.doNotSendWarning: Only tokens managed by the Vaipakam protocol are shown here. Do not send any tokens directly to your vault address — they may not be recoverable.
vaultAssets.connectBody: Connect your wallet to view your vault holdings.
vaultAssets.switchChainBody: Switch your wallet to a Vaipakam-supported chain to view your vault holdings.

vaultRecover.pageSubtitle: Recover ERC-20 tokens that landed in your vault proxy outside the protocol deposit flow. Read the Advanced User Guide section first — declaring an incorrect source can lock your vault under the protocol's sanctions policy.
vaultRecover.sourceHint: The address that originally sent these tokens. By declaring it here you assert that it belongs to you or was authorized by you.
vaultRecover.warning1: If the source address is on the sanctions list, your vault will be locked. The lock auto-lifts only if the address is later removed from the sanctions oracle.
vaultRecover.warning2: Tokens are returned to YOUR connected wallet — recipient is hardcoded. No third-party recipient possible.
vaultRecover.warning3: If you did NOT send these tokens yourself, do not recover. Unsolicited dust does not affect your protocol balance.
vaultRecover.modalWarningSanctions: Declaring a sanctions-listed source will lock your vault until that source is removed from the oracle. This is the same lock the protocol applies to any sanctioned address.
vaultRecover.modalWarningOwnership: By signing, you assert under EIP-712 that the source you declared belongs to a wallet you control or authorized.
vaultRecover.bannedBody: The source you declared ({{source}}) is on the sanctions list. Your Vaipakam Vault has been locked. Tokens stayed in the vault — no movement.
vaultRecover.bannedAutoUnlock: The lock auto-lifts if the address is later removed from the sanctions oracle.
vaultRecover.errOutcomeMissing: Transaction succeeded but no recovery event was found in the receipt. Try refreshing the page.
vaultRecover.connectBody: Connect your wallet to use the stuck-token recovery flow.
vaultRecover.switchChainBody: Switch your wallet to a Vaipakam-supported chain to use the recovery flow.

indexerBadge.caughtUpHeading: Indexer caught up to the chain
indexerBadge.caughtUpBody: The backend indexer has read up to the chain's most recent finalised (safe) block. The page is rendering the latest cached state. Transactions submitted now will see exactly what the page shows.
indexerBadge.catchingUpBody: The indexer is a few blocks behind the chain head and is actively catching up. Recently-emitted offers / loans / activity may not appear on the page yet. New rows will fill in as the cursor advances on the next cron tick (every ~1 minute).
indexerBadge.behindBody: The indexer has fallen far behind the chain head. Either the watcher cron is stuck or a chain spike caused a backlog. Treat the page as a stale snapshot — operator action may be required. The cursor will resume advancing once the backlog clears.
indexerBadge.liveBody: The indexer cache is unreachable, so your browser is fetching every offer / loan / activity row directly from the chain. The data is always authoritative, but pages take longer to load. Please wait for the page to finish loading before submitting any transaction — a tx that depends on data still being fetched might act on a partial view.
indexerBadge.localDevBody: Your wallet is connected to a local Anvil or Hardhat node (chain id 31337). The cloud indexer can't reach local dev nodes, so the page reads directly from your local RPC. This is normal during development — no action needed.
indexerBadge.safeBlockFootnote: "Safe block" = the most recent block the chain considers final under its consensus rules. Reorgs older than this are exceedingly rare on rollups, so we read up to that point and ignore the live tip until it finalises.
indexerBadge.liveRpcInSyncHeading: Reading live from the chain — in sync
indexerBadge.liveRpcInSyncBody: The indexer cache is unreachable, so the page is fetching every offer / loan / activity row directly from the chain via RPC. The latest finalised (safe) block has been read recently, so the page is up-to-date. Submitting transactions while green is safe — what the page shows is what's on chain.
indexerBadge.loadingBody: The page is fetching its initial data — offers, loans, activity. Wait for this to settle before submitting a transaction; a tx that depends on data still being fetched might act on a partial view. When it finishes you'll see the freshness state (Live / Catching up / Behind).
indexerBadge.liveUpdatingBody: Everything on this page is fresh to the latest finalised block. A refresh is in flight right now (someone created an offer or a loan landed on chain), so a row or two more may appear in a moment. Safe to act on what's shown — the refresh only adds, it doesn't invalidate.
indexerBadge.liveSafeBlockTooltip: Polling the chain's safe head every 2 s while this popover is open — the number ticks up as blocks are finalised.

chainDiagnostics.purgeConfirm: Purge all browser-side state for this site (IndexedDB, localStorage, sessionStorage)? Connected wallets and saved preferences will be cleared. The page will need a reload.
